docs.title: Python API reference (mkdocstrings)
docs.text: The blocks below document selected Python modules used by the gateway and vault layer.

docs.title: db_root() -> Path
docs.text:
Directorio db/ del monorepo.

Si el proceso arranca con cwd distinto del repo (p. ej. services/db-writer), definir DUCKCLAW_REPO_ROOT apuntando a la raíz del monorepo; si no, se usa Path("db").resolve() relativo al cwd (comportamiento legacy).

docs.title: shared_tenant_dir(tenant_id: Any) -> Path
docs.text: Directorio db/shared/{tenant_slug}/ (mkdir incluso si aún no hay .duckdb).

docs.title: validate_user_db_path(user_id: Any, db_path: str, tenant_id: Any | None = None) -> bool
docs.text:
Acepta rutas .duckdb bajo el árbol db/ del repo, con comprobación por usuario cuando la ruta está bajo private/ o shared/ (no en la raíz de db/).

Archivos en la raíz de db/ (p. ej. duckclaw.duckdb, ACL del gateway) son válidos para el consumidor singleton (mensajes ya filtrados en el Gateway).

docs.title: vault_scope_id_for_tenant(tenant_id: Any) -> str
docs.text: Ámbito de registro multi-bóveda alineado con el tenant efectivo del gateway. default o vacío → legacy (una sola familia de filas con scope_id '').

docs.title: duckclaw.integrations.telegram.telegram_webhook_multiplex
docs.text: Multiplexación de un solo URL de webhook para varios bots (secret_token → worker + token).

docs.title: telegram_webhook_header_fingerprint(header_value: str | None) -> str
docs.text: Identificador corto para dedupe (distinto por bot / secreto).

docs.title: telegram_webhook_resolve_dispatch(header_value: str | None, *, default_worker_id: str, default_tenant_id: str, default_bot_token: str) -> Literal['reject'] | TelegramWebhookResolvedDispatch | tuple[Literal['legacy_default'], str, str, str]
docs.text:
Resuelve worker, tenant y token de salida.

Retorna: - reject: 403 - TelegramWebhookResolvedDispatch: ruta multiplex explícita - ("legacy_default", worker, tenant, token): cabecera coincide con TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET o no hay rutas y el modo clásico aplica

docs.title: telegram_webhook_route_bindings() -> tuple[list[TelegramWebhookRouteBinding], str | None]
docs.text: Cachea el parseo de DUCKCLAW_TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_ROUTES. Retorna (lista, error_parseo); si error_parseo no es None, la lista puede estar vacía.

docs.title: DB Writer API Reference
docs.text: This page has two parts: write-path contract (for operators and integrators) and Python API reference (mkdocstrings).

docs.title: Write-path contract
docs.text:
• Only services/db-writer should apply durable mutations to DuckDB in production (singleton writer).
  • The gateway and agents enqueue intents (SQL/state deltas) via Redis; the writer consumes, runs transactional work, and updates task status.
  • For HTTP ingress, POST /api/v1/db/write on the API Gateway accepts payloads that target a specific db_path and user_id when using path-aware routing; the writer executes against that path under safety rules.

Related:

  • Singleton Writer — architecture contract.
  • Finanz Admin SQL DB-Writer — spec pointer (canonical file in specs/features/).
  • Operations / Commands — PM2 and uv run python services/db-writer/main.py for local runs.

docs.title: duckclaw.db_write_queue
docs.text:
Cola singleton de escrituras DuckDB (Redis) y confirmación por task_id.

Usado por admin_sql (poll), db-writer (SET task_status), y war rooms en modo RO.

docs.title: DbWriteTaskStatus
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Estado publicado por db-writer tras ejecutar (o fallar) una escritura.

docs.title: enqueue_duckdb_write_sync(*, db_path: str, query: str, params: list[Any] | None = None, user_id: str = 'default', tenant_id: str = 'default', task_id: str | None = None, queue_name: str = DEFAULT_WRITE_QUEUE_NAME) -> str
docs.text: LPUSH del payload JSON. Devuelve task_id.

docs.title: poll_task_status_sync(task_id: str, *, timeout_sec: float = 3.0, interval_sec: float = 0.05) -> DbWriteTaskStatus | None
docs.text: GET task_status: hasta timeout. None si no hubo confirmación.

docs.title: duckclaw.gateway_db
docs.text:
Ruta y acceso a la BD del API Gateway (microservicio services/api-gateway).

Usado por duckclaw.graphs.graph_server, forge, workers y scripts cuando necesitan la misma DuckDB que usa el Gateway. Resuelve desde DUCKCLAW_*_DB_PATH (multiplex) y DUCKDB_PATH; no usa DUCKCLAW_DB_PATH (eliminada).

docs.title: GatewayDbEphemeralReadonly
docs.text: Acceso RO al archivo del gateway sin mantener duckdb.connect abierto entre llamadas. Compatible con código que usa .query, ._path y ._read_only (p. ej. append_task_audit → cola).

docs.title: get_gateway_db() -> Any
docs.text:
Facade RO efímera a la misma ruta que el API Gateway (sin conexión persistente al archivo).

Preferir pasar la conexión de la bóveda activa cuando el contexto sea multi-vault.

docs.title: get_gateway_db_path() -> str
docs.text:
Ruta absoluta del DuckDB del gateway (hub ACL / whitelist).

Primera variable no vacía entre DUCKCLAW_WAR_ROOM_ACL_DB_PATH, DUCKCLAW_FINANZ_DB_PATH, DUCKCLAW_JOB_HUNTER_DB_PATH, DUCKCLAW_SIATA_DB_PATH, luego DUCKDB_PATH; resuelta con resolve_env_duckdb_path.

docs.title: get_war_room_acl_db_path() -> str
docs.text:
DuckDB donde vive war_room_core.wr_members para zero-trust en War Rooms.

Si DUCKCLAW_WAR_ROOM_ACL_DB_PATH está definida (p. ej. finanzdb1 mientras el grafo del gateway usa jobhunterdb1), las comprobaciones WR leen esa ruta en solo lectura. Si no, coincide con get_gateway_db_path().

docs.title: raw_gateway_db_path_from_environ() -> str
docs.text: Primera variable de entorno no vacía en GATEWAY_DB_ENV_KEYS; fallback db/duckclaw.duckdb.

docs.title: raw_gateway_db_path_from_mapping(mapping: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str
docs.text: Igual que raw_gateway_db_path_from_environ pero leyendo un dict (p. ej. apps[].env del PM2 JSON).

docs.title: resolve_env_duckdb_path(raw: str) -> str
docs.text:
Absolutiza una ruta de archivo DuckDB.

Las rutas relativas (p. ej. DUCKCLAW_FINANZ_DB_PATH=db/private/.../x.duckdb) se resuelven contra DUCKCLAW_REPO_ROOT, no contra el cwd del proceso (PM2 puede arrancar fuera del repo y abrir otra copia del archivo).

docs.title: Singleton Writer Contract
docs.text: DuckClaw enforces a single mutation path: services/db-writer is the only component allowed to write DuckDB state.

docs.title: Why It Exists
docs.text:
• Guarantees ACID transaction boundaries for all state deltas.
  • Reduces race conditions across concurrent chat/tool executions.
  • Centralizes idempotency, retries, and audit status updates.

docs.title: Write Flow
docs.text:
1. Gateway/agents generate a validated state delta or SQL write intent.
  2. Intent is enqueued in Redis.
  3. db-writer consumes the queue, runs transactional writes, and publishes task status.

docs.title: Scope Boundaries
docs.text:
• Gateway and workers are read-oriented by default.
  • Write permissions are not distributed to template workers.
  • Any new mutation path must remain compatible with the singleton contract.

docs.title: Strix Sandbox
docs.text: The Strix sandbox isolates untrusted or tool-generated execution from core gateway processes.

docs.title: Security Objectives
docs.text:
• Minimize privilege surface for generated code execution.
  • Prevent direct access to sensitive host resources.
  • Keep deterministic audit traces for sandbox operations.

docs.title: Practical Constraints
docs.text:
• Network and filesystem exposure are explicitly constrained.
  • Outputs are passed back through controlled channels.
  • Gateway responses must not claim artifacts that sandbox did not actually produce.

docs.title: Related Operations
docs.text: See also Strix Sandbox Security for operational controls, hardening checklists, and troubleshooting.

docs.title: Tri-Cameral Memory
docs.text:
DuckClaw models memory in three complementary layers:

  • SQL: deterministic financial/accounting state and operational records.
  • PGQ: graph-like relationships for multi-hop entity context.
  • VSS: semantic recall for context injection and retrieval-augmented interactions.

docs.title: Design Goals
docs.text:
• Preserve deterministic writes for ledger-critical tasks.
  • Support relationship traversal without overloading transactional tables.
  • Enable fast semantic recall over contextual artifacts.

docs.title: Operational Notes
docs.text:
• Tenant/user vault resolution keeps private and shared scopes separated.
  • Semantic context ingestion is asynchronous and queue-backed.
  • Worker prompts should treat SQL as hard truth for balances/totals.

docs.title: Operational quick reference (gateway)
docs.text:
The API Gateway ingests photos and visual documents for Telegram (and related paths) before the manager graph runs. Typical backends: MLX (mlx_vlm in-process or MLX HTTP) → Gemini when a key is set → OpenAI vision only if explicitly enabled.

Variable Role GEMINI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY Gemini VLM; also DUCKCLAW_VLM_GEMINI_API_KEY. OPENAI_API_KEY Vision only with DUCKCLAW_VLM_ALLOW_OPENAI_VISION=1. DUCKCLAW_VLM_PRIMARY mlx (default) or openai / cloud / openai_first. DUCKCLAW_VLM_ALLOW_OPENAI_VISION Must be truthy to use OpenAI for vision. DUCKCLAW_VLM_DISABLE_LOCAL_MLX_VLM, VLM_MLX_DISABLE_LOCAL, DUCKCLAW_VLM_MLX_DISABLE_LOCAL Disable in-process mlx_vlm when set truthy.

If Gemini returns 503, users may get a short Telegram notice suggesting retry or local MLX (Gemma VLM / mlx_vlm).

Full Spanish runbook (Redis, Telegram, PM2, tables above expanded): Commands (COMANDOS) §5.2.
